title: The RN mental model
description: What is React Native, and what's different from web React?

questions.prompt: React Native renders your components as:
questions.explanation: Your <View> becomes UIView on iOS and android.view.View on Android.

questions.prompt: You DON'T use these primitives in RN:

questions.explanation: RN throws if raw strings are direct children of <View>.
Subset of CSS: no cascades, no selectors, no `%` widths cascade like web.

options: Same as web with one default: flexDirection defaults to 'column'
questions.explanation: Column-first because vertical is native for mobile UIs.
